dna.dataSources.entity.title: JDBC Data Source Definition
dna.dataSources.entity.description: Manages JDBC data source connection settings and runtime connection status.
dna.dataSources.title.name: Data Source Name
dna.dataSources.description.name: Display name of the data source
dna.dataSources.description.code: Unique business code of the data source
dna.dataSources.description.driverId: Primary key of the related JDBC driver definition
dna.dataSources.description.driverCode: Business code of the related driver
dna.dataSources.description.jdbcUrl: Full JDBC URL, for example jdbc:mysql://host:3306/demo
dna.dataSources.description.connectionPr: Optional extra JDBC properties in Java Properties format
dna.dataSources.description.enabled: Whether the data source can be used by runtime logic
dna.dataSources.description.lastConnectS: Status of the latest connection test
dna.dataSources.description.lastConnectM: Result message of the latest connection test
dna.dataSources.description.lastTestedAt: Time of the latest connection test
dna.dataSources.description.databaseProd: Database product name returned by the latest successful connection
Database version returned by the latest successful connection
dna.dataSources.page.form.driver.descrip: Create a driver on the Driver Management page first.
dna.dataSources.page.warning.selectDataS: Select a data source to test.
dna.dataSources.page.error.connect: Failed to connect to the data source.
dna.dataSources.page.alert.noDrivers.des: Create and download a JDBC driver on the Driver Management page first, then come back to configure the data source.
